content:
# Final Review Votes
**Producer:** agent:claude/claude-opus-4-6

## Summary

All four amendments are accepted. Amendments fr-8-88f788ba and fr-8-02afe4b6 identify the same genuine bug: Phase 2 persistence failures at lines 782-788 are logged as non-blocking warnings, but Phase 3 at lines 801-831 still iterates outcomes checking only `outcome.verified`, not whether the durable write succeeded. The comment at line 798-800 ('verified records are already durable') is a lie when Phase 2 failed, enabling duplicate comments on replay. Amendment fr-8-dc90abe2 correctly identifies duplicated comment-formatting logic between success_reconciliation.rs (lines 807-810) and planned_elsewhere.rs (lines 306-308) — identical format strings that must be kept in sync manually. Amendment fr-8-f4e46f89 correctly identifies that existing_keys (line 884) is built once and never updated during the reconstruction loop, so the same (finding_summary, mapped_to_bead_id) pair present across multiple final-review aggregates will bypass dedup and be reconstructed multiple times.

## Votes

- `fr-8-88f788ba`: Accept
  Verified at lines 772-831. Phase 2 (lines 782-794) wraps the record_planned_elsewhere_mapping call in `if let Err(e)` and logs the failure as non-blocking, then continues. Phase 3 (lines 801-831) gates commenting only on `outcome.verified`, not on whether Phase 2 actually persisted the verified state. When Phase 2 fails but Phase 3 succeeds, a restart finds the mapping still unverified in the journal, re-verifies, and posts a duplicate comment. The proposed fix of tracking Phase 2 per-mapping success in a HashSet and gating Phase 3 on it is correct and straightforward.
- `fr-8-dc90abe2`: Accept
  Confirmed: success_reconciliation.rs line 807-810 uses `format!("Planned-elsewhere mapping from {}: {}", outcome.mapping.active_bead_id, outcome.mapping.finding_summary)` and planned_elsewhere.rs lines 306-308 uses the identical format with `mapping.active_bead_id` and `mapping.finding_summary`. The success_reconciliation path calls verify_and_comment_mappings with post_comments=false then reimplements commenting separately. This is a real maintenance hazard — either extracting a shared function or adding cross-referencing comments is appropriate.
- `fr-8-02afe4b6`: Accept
  This identifies the same real bug as fr-8-88f788ba with precise line references. Lines 782-788 show record_planned_elsewhere_mapping can fail, and lines 803-813 show commenting proceeds regardless. The Phase 3 comment at line 798-800 claiming 'verified records are already durable' is incorrect when Phase 2 failed. Accepting because it is a genuine correctness defect — duplicate comments on replay when persistence fails.
- `fr-8-f4e46f89`: Accept
  Verified at lines 883-948. `existing_keys` is built once at line 884 from `existing_mappings` and is never mutated inside the loop that iterates all final-review aggregates (line 894) and their amendments (line 907). If two different aggregates contain the same (normalized_body, mapped_to_bead_id), the dedup check at line 920 passes both times since existing_keys still only contains the original journal entries. This produces duplicate reconstructed mappings that will be verified and commented multiple times. The fix of inserting newly reconstructed keys into the dedup set is correct.
